accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and verified context (PHS Act §546, FDA approval standard, sponsors, committee, figures).

no_hallucinations.why: The FDA reference is accurate added context (the FD&C Act governs the approval/clearance standard) and nothing is fabricated.

states_purpose.why: The summary clearly states the bill reauthorizes and updates the first responder training program under PHS Act §546.

changes_to_existing_law.why: It correctly identifies the amendments to Section 546 of the Public Health Service Act.

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill contains no material exceptions, conditions, or carve-outs to summarize.

effective_dates.why: It notes the funding authorization period of fiscal years 2026 through 2030 and the introduction date.

major_provisions_covered.why: It captures the scope broadening beyond opioids, the marketing-standard change, the Tribal capitalization, and the funding update.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary is well-organized and internally consistent.

concise.why: It is appropriately concise without padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: PHS Act §546, FDA, the three sponsors, and the HELP Committee are all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: The $36,000,000 (FY2019-2023), $57,000,000 (FY2026-2030), and July 30, 2025 date all match the bill text.
